operating_state: Total revenue increased 8% to $8.7 billion driven by semiconductor solutions growth from strong networking, server storage and broadband demand, partially offset by lower wireless product demand and pricing pressures. Infrastructure software grew modestly from mainframe solutions increases offset by lower FC SAN demand. Apple represented 18% of revenue with secured multi-year RF and wireless component supply agreements while top customers accounted for 35% of total revenue.
cost_structure: Gross margin expanded to 70% from 67% primarily due to lower amortization of acquisition-related intangible assets from the 2016 Broadcom Corporation acquisition, partially offset by less favorable product mix and continued semiconductor pricing pressures. Operating expenses increased modestly with higher stock-based compensation from annual equity awards at higher fair values and VMware merger-related costs, offset by lower variable compensation.
material_events: Generated $4.5 billion in operating cash flow, repurchased $2.8 billion of common stock, and paid $1.9 billion in cash dividends with quarterly dividend declared at $4.60 per share. VMware stockholders adopted the $61 billion merger agreement with transaction expected to close in fiscal 2023 subject to regulatory approvals. California Institute of Technology patent litigation resulted in $270.2 million judgment though appellate court vacated damages and ordered new trial.
macro_exposures: Heightened exposure to U.S.-China trade tensions including potential tariffs and export restrictions affecting customer access and competitive positioning. Tax legislation changes present material risks including GILTI deduction reduction beginning fiscal 2027, new book minimum tax starting fiscal 2024, and OECD global minimum tax proposals. Singapore tax incentives expire in 2025 and Malaysia tax holiday expires in fiscal 2028 potentially increasing corporate tax rates.
forward_outlook: VMware merger completion anticipated in fiscal 2023 with $32 billion in committed debt financing and integration requiring significant management attention across customer relationships and systems. Management expects higher capital expenditures with continued substantial research and development investments to address rapid technological changes. Stock repurchase programs totaling $20 billion authorized through December 2023 with $9 billion remaining available, subject to cash flow priorities.
uncertainty_profile: VMware merger subject to Hart-Scott-Rodino antitrust clearance and European Union regulatory approvals with Korean Fair Trade Commission investigation ongoing. Transfer pricing policies face ongoing scrutiny from tax authorities across multiple jurisdictions with potential adjustments. Intellectual property litigation exposure includes customer indemnification obligations and potential treble damages for willful infringement findings with substantial customer concentration risk from potential loss of top customers.
